title: Set --cpu-rt-runtime=950000 automatically at podman level on machine restart
body:
/kind feature

**Description**

Unable to find a  consistent way to specify the CPU real-time at podman level as podman is not a daemon. For e.g. in docker we were able to set at following at the docker daemon level:

`--cpu-rt-runtime=950000`

However, in Podman there is no way and because of this, we are unable to set CPU RT runtime at Podman level.

**Describe the results you received:**

Containers created with CPU Real-time are not able start as /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu,cpuacct/machine.slice directory doesn't exist on machine restart.

**Describe the results you expected:**

We should be able to set /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu,cpuacct/machine.slice/cpu.rt_runtime_us on machine restart.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

As a workaround, we are starting machine.service on a Podman machine restart from a container service:
```
# cat rt.sh
/bin/systemctl start machine.slice
/bin/echo 950000 > /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu,cpuacct/machine.slice/cpu.rt_runtime_us

cat /etc/systemd/system/testc.service
 
[Unit]
Description=Podman container-testc.service
Documentation=man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
Wants=network.target
After=network-online.target
RequiresMountsFor=%t/containers
 
[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
Restart=on-failure
TimeoutStopSec=70
ExecStartPre=/bin/rt.sh
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman start testc
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop --ignore --cidfile %t/container-testc.ctr-id -t 10
PIDFile=%t/container-testc.pid
Type=forking
 
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target
```

Is there a better way to handle this situation?

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.3
API Version:  3.2.3
Go Version:   go1.15.14
Built:        Thu Sep 23 22:03:03 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


NO

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
> /bin/echo 950000 > /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu,cpuacct/machine.slice/cpu.rt_runtime_us

this affects every cgroup under `/sys/fs/cgroup/cpu,cpuacct/machine.slice` so not only Podman containers.

For this reason, I don't think Podman should touch it.

Would it be cleaner if the ` /bin/echo 950000 > /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu,cpuacct/machine.slice/cpu.rt_runtime_us` command is moved to a systemd oneshot service on its own and add a dependency to it?
This seems like a system configuration issue, not a Podman issue. I am going to close, but we can continue the conversation here.
